attempts.test_code:
def test_bitcount_multiple_ones():
    # 127 in binary is 0b1111111 (7 ones)
    # The buggy version uses XOR (n ^= n - 1) which doesn't clear the lowest set bit
    # causing an infinite loop or incorrect result, while the fixed version uses AND (n &= n - 1)
    assert bitcount(127) == 7

attempts.test_code:
from source import bitcount

def test_bitcount_multiple_ones():
    # 127 in binary is 0b1111111 (7 ones)
    # The buggy version uses n ^= n - 1 which doesn't clear the lowest set bit,
    # causing an infinite loop (or very long loop for large numbers)
    # The fixed version should return 7 for bitcount(127)
    assert bitcount(127) == 7, f"bitcount(127) should be 7, got {bitcount(127)}"
